content.text: Upscaling reconstructs lower resolutions to the native resolution using advanced algorithms, improving image quality and performance.
When ENBSeries is active, only DLAA is available.

content.help: NVIDIA DLSS 4 is automatically used if available, otherwise AMD FSR 3.1 is used. (Default: DLSS)
Controls sharpening. DLSS uses NVIDIA Image Scaling sharpen; FSR uses RCAS. (Default: 0.2)
Uses NVIDIA DLSS/FSR Frame Generation when supported. Reflex is enabled automatically while active. (Default: Disabled)
Controls the DLSS generated frames between rendered frames. Unsupported values are clamped by the NVIDIA runtime. (Default: 1 generated frame / 2x)
Controls NVIDIA Reflex low latency mode. DLSS Frame Generation forces at least On while active. (Default: On)
Controls the DLSS model preset. Recommended uses K for DLAA/Quality/Balanced, M for Performance, and L for Ultra Performance. Default lets NVIDIA choose. (Default: Recommended)
Shows lightweight DX12 swapchain stats while DLSS or FSR is active. (Default: Disabled)
Shows the current DX12 upscaler inputs as a 2x2 debug view: original color, depth, motion vectors, and final image. (Default: Disabled)

content.text: Dynamic MFG requires support from your GPU, driver, and NVIDIA runtime. If unsupported, the mod falls back to regular DLSS Frame Generation.

content.help: Lets NVIDIA Streamline dynamically choose the generated frame multiplier when the runtime reports support. (Default: Disabled)
Target output frame rate for Dynamic MFG. Use 0 to let Streamline auto-detect the display refresh rate. (Default: 300)
